nodes.label: Runtime Host Bootstrap
nodes.summary: Main entry mounts simulated host and chooses desktop/mobile runtime.
nodes.details: Entry wiring sets root mount, strips legacy query params, creates registry and host, then mounts initial system.

nodes.label: Command Tokenization + Local Fast-Path Gate
nodes.summary: Shell tokenizes command and routes local commands without worker call.
nodes.details: Unknown commands branch into LLM processing path; local commands execute in-shell.

nodes.label: LLM Request Builder + Turnstile Token
nodes.summary: Client resolves Turnstile token and POSTs structured shell payload to /worker/.
nodes.details: Payload includes command fields, cwd, vfs snapshot, and security/session attributes.

nodes.label: Worker Request Guards
nodes.summary: Worker validates body shape, origin policy, and rate limits.
nodes.details: Before LLM invocation the request passes parse/validate and multi-window rate-limiter checks.

nodes.label: Turnstile Verification
nodes.summary: Worker verifies Turnstile token and allowed hostname before continuing.
nodes.details: Failure path returns a guarded shell error with 403.

nodes.label: Recognized Executable Gate
nodes.summary: Unknown tokens short-circuit to deterministic command-not-found response.
nodes.details: This prevents unnecessary LLM calls for unsupported command tokens.

nodes.label: System Prompt Assembly + Workers AI Call
nodes.summary: Worker constructs deterministic shell prompt and calls env.AI.run.
nodes.details: System message defines strict JSON-only shell adapter behavior and output schema.

nodes.label: Normalize + Enforce Response Limits
nodes.summary: Model output is parsed/normalized then sanitized under strict caps.
nodes.details: Unsafe mutations/events/suggestions are dropped or truncated before return.

nodes.label: Client Parse + Output Rendering
nodes.summary: Shell parses response, prints stdout/stderr, and shows suggestions.
nodes.details: Response body is consumed before mutation/event processing.

nodes.label: Mutation Guard + Overlay Apply
nodes.summary: Client sanitizes LLM mutations and applies allowed overlay filesystem changes.
nodes.details: Dropped/truncated mutation diagnostics are surfaced in shell output.

nodes.label: UI Event Dispatch
nodes.summary: Client executes LLM UI events (open paths/apps) through guarded handlers.
nodes.details: Open commands are executed via app launch or path open dispatch.

edges.label: Ubuntu profile active
edges.details: Desktop host mounts Ubuntu shell profile; command loop starts.

edges.label: POST /worker/
edges.details: Client sends command context with token and session id.

edges.label: valid payload + limits
edges.details: Only valid requests proceed to cryptographic proof verification.

edges.label: proof accepted
edges.details: Known executable check decides whether to call LLM.

edges.label: recognized token
edges.details: Recognized commands continue into LLM prompt + model run.

edges.label: model candidate
edges.details: Model output is normalized then constrained under worker response limits.

edges.label: JSON shell payload
edges.details: Final sanitized payload returned to client terminal.

edges.label: vfs_mutations[]
edges.details: Mutations pass through client-side guard and overlay filesystem apply.

edges.label: ui_events[]
edges.details: UI events are executed asynchronously after render/mutation processing.
